access.comment: key=access sums BOTH rules (correct per-key view)

math: 1200 B/event. execve typ 50000 -> $9.12/mo. modules typ 50 -> ~$0.01/mo. Total/per-key(access) typ ~$9.13/mo. The two are distinct rules sharing one key.

naive_wrong_model.comment: A model that treats one key = one rule conflates the two rules.
naive_wrong_model.error: If the impl assumes a key uniquely identifies a rule, it either (a) drops one rule's volume (counts only the first execve, missing modules, or vice versa) or (b) mis-attributes the combined per-key volume to a single (wrong) rule's tier. Per-key aggregation MAY sum (correct), but per-RULE tiering must keep execve=HIGH and modules=LOW separate.
